title: Abigail Movie: Release Date, Cast & Production Details
text: The vampire horror film Abigail debuted in theaters on April 19, 2024, nearly 88 years after the original Dracula's Daughter (1936). The movie received an exclusive theatrical release through Universal Pictures. Following its cinema run, it became available on Peacock for four months before moving to Amazon Prime Video for streaming. The film reimagines the 1936 classic as a modern-day story centered on a 12-year-old ballerina. A group of kidnappers abduct Abigail, demanding a $50 million ransom from her powerful father. Confined in an isolated mansion, the captors discover their young hostage is actually a vampire. As described in the film's official synopsis: Children can be such monsters. The kidnappers find themselves trapped with an seemingly invincible creature possessing classic vampire weaknesses. The cast includes Alisha Weir as Abigail (the titular vampire), Melissa Barrera as Joey, Giancarlo Esposito as the kidnapping group's leader, Kathryn Newton as Sammy, Dan Stevens as Frank, Kevin Durand as Peter, William Catlett as Rickles, and Angus Cloud as Dean (his final performance). Directors Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett collaborated with writers Guy Busick and Stephen Shields, drawing from their successful work on the Scream franchise and Ready or Not.
